title: Symptoms of shigellosis (Shigella) - Canada.ca
content:
## Symptoms of shigellosis ( Shigella )
Learn about the symptoms of shigellosis and what to do if you become ill.
- Symptoms of shigellosis
- What to do if you become ill

## Symptoms of shigellosis
Shigellosis has a wide range of symptoms. You may not get sick at all. But if you do, symptoms usually start within 1 to 3 days after exposure.
- diarrhea (watery and often bloody)
Most symptoms end within 5 to 7 days. Most people recover fully, though it may take several weeks to months before bowel habits return to normal.

## What to do if you become ill
If you think you have shigellosis, see your health care provider.
Avoid preparing food for other people if you think you are:
- sick with shigellosis
- suffering from any other contagious illness causing diarrhea
Severe vomiting and diarrhea can lead to dehydration (loss of fluids). To protect against dehydration, drink plenty of fluids.
